# Salty Horizon Exclusive Scuba Diving

> Private, personalized PADI scuba diving in Tamarindo, Costa Rica, with instructors Camille Segonne (PADI OWSI, MSDT) and Julien Escales (PADI OWSI). Trilingual: English, French, Spanish.

## Pages

- [Home](https://www.saltyhorizondiving.com/): overview, about the instructors, gallery, guest reviews, booking form, contact details
- [Experiences](https://www.saltyhorizondiving.com/experiences.html): Intro to Scuba (Pool) from $80, Discover Scuba Diving from $170, Fun Dive at the Catalina Islands from $130, Fun Dive at the Bat Islands (bull sharks, contact for pricing), Private Snorkeling from $80
- [PADI Courses](https://www.saltyhorizondiving.com/padi-courses.html): PADI Open Water Diver certification from $540, PADI Advanced Open Water Diver from $500
- [Private Charters](https://www.saltyhorizondiving.com/private-charters.html): fully customized private boat days combining scuba, snorkeling, paddle boarding, e-foil and jet ski
- [Scuba Diving in Tamarindo FAQ](https://www.saltyhorizondiving.com/scuba-diving-tamarindo-faq.html): 43 questions and answers on diving at the Catalina Islands and Bat Islands, marine life, manta ray and whale season, diving conditions, certification and booking

## Other languages

The pages above are also available in full, natively, in French and Spanish, at their own URLs:
- French: https://www.saltyhorizondiving.com/fr/ (and /fr/experiences.html, /fr/padi-courses.html, /fr/private-charters.html, /fr/scuba-diving-tamarindo-faq.html)
- Spanish: https://www.saltyhorizondiving.com/es/ (and /es/experiences.html, /es/padi-courses.html, /es/private-charters.html, /es/scuba-diving-tamarindo-faq.html)

## Journal (blog)

Sourced articles on diving, marine life and Tamarindo, in English only: [The Salty Horizon Journal](https://www.saltyhorizondiving.com/blog/index.html)

- [Where to Dive in Costa Rica: Comparing Its Very Different Underwater Worlds](https://www.saltyhorizondiving.com/blog/costa-rica-dive-destinations-comparison.html): Isla del Coco, Caño Island, the Catalinas and the Bat Islands compared — distance, cost, certification requirements and what you'll actually see.
- [The Scientists Tracking Costa Rica's Giant Manta Rays](https://www.saltyhorizondiving.com/blog/mantas-costa-rica-manta-ray-research.html): inside the Mantas Costa Rica research project — acoustic telemetry, photo-ID and satellite tags, and why the species was just uplisted to Endangered.
- [What to Do in Tamarindo Beyond Diving](https://www.saltyhorizondiving.com/blog/things-to-do-tamarindo-beyond-diving.html): for the non-diver in your group — leatherback turtles at Las Baulas National Marine Park, surf breaks, mangrove estuary kayaking, sunset sailing.
- [How to Photograph Whitetip Reef Sharks and Sea Turtles](https://www.saltyhorizondiving.com/blog/photographing-whitetip-sharks-sea-turtles.html): a sourced guide to shooting Tamarindo's two most-photographed, most-forgiving dive subjects responsibly.
- [The Sea Turtles You Might Meet Diving Off Tamarindo](https://www.saltyhorizondiving.com/blog/sea-turtles-tamarindo-species-guide.html): olive ridley, green and hawksbill turtles — diet, nesting season and habits.

## Facts

- Location: Tamarindo, Guanacaste, Costa Rica
- Contact: +506 8775 9641 (WhatsApp), saltyhorizon.scuba@gmail.com
- Languages spoken: English, French, Spanish
- Main dive site: Catalina Islands (Islas Catalinas), Gulf of Papagayo, about 40 minutes by boat from Tamarindo
- Advanced dive site: Bat Islands (Islas Murciélago), known for bull shark encounters, May to November, Advanced Open Water certification required
